title: aconitum × teppneri nothosubsp. goetzii
intro: Aconitum × teppneri nothosubsp. goetzii, also known as Goetz's monk's hood, is a nothosubspecies of the Aconitum × teppneri plant, a hybrid of the Aconitum vulparia and Aconitum variegatum species. It contains aconitine, a toxic alkaloid with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat pain, inflammation, and fever, as well as to induce sweating and promote digestion. The health benefits of Goetz's monk's hood include its potential to reduce muscle spasms, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, although its toxicity requires careful handling and dosage. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically dried and used in infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, but caution is advised due to the risk of overdose and toxicity.
